articles.title: Gold remains depressed as hot US CPI lifts Fed rate hike bets and supports USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a negative bias for the second straight day on Wednesday, although it manages to hold above a multi-day low, around the $4,638 region, touched the previous day.

articles.expand: In case you missed it: US April CPI 3.8% y/y vs 3.7% expected It was no surprise that headline inflation got another bump up on the back of higher energy prices. articles.title: New Zealand Inflation Expectations Jump as RBNZ OCR Outlook Turns Hawkish
articles.expand: New Zealand’s latest RBNZ Survey of Expectations showed a sharp rise in near-term inflation expectations, reinforcing concerns that price pressures may remain elevated even as growth slows. articles.expand: The OECD secretary-general said the BOJ is not clearly behind the curve on rates and called for reform of international trading rules, describing the upcoming Trump-Xi summit as an important part of that process. articles.title: OECD Sees BoJ Raising Rates to 2% by End-2027
articles.expand: The OECD projected on Wednesday that the Bank of Japan will continue steadily normalizing monetary policy, with the short-term policy rate expected to rise from the current 0.75% to 2% by the end of 2027. In its latest Economic Survey of Japan, the OECD argued that resilient domestic demand, sustained wage growth, and improving inflation […] ...

articles.title: Japanese Yen remains subdued despite hawkish tone surrounding BoJ’s policy outlook
articles.expand: USD/JPY extends its gains for the third successive day, trading around 157.70 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: OECD projects BoJ hiking rates to 2% by end-2027
articles.expand: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) projected the Bank of Japan (BoJ) will raise its policy rate to 2.0% by end-2027 from 0.75% currently, supported by higher inflation expectations, solid wage growth and a closed output gap.

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Inflation Is Moving the Wrong Way, Labor Market Is Not
articles.expand: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee warned overnight that US inflation is moving in the “wrong way,” expressing particular concern that price pressures are spreading beyond energy and tariffs into broader service categories. His remarks followed April’s firmer-than-expected CPI report, which showed both headline (3.8%) and core (2.8%) inflation a...

articles.title: Australian Dollar edges higher to near 0.7250 on hawkish RBA tone, eyes on Trump-Xi summit
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair gains ground to around 0.7240 during the Asian trading hours on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) amid a hawkish stance from the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA).

articles.title: Asia open: US inflation reaccelerates to 3.8%, and chip stocks falter
articles.expand: US inflation surged to 3.8% in April, the highest level in three years, wiping out Federal Reserve rate cut expectations and pushing Treasury yields and the US dollar higher. articles.title: Canadian Dollar flat lines near 1.3700 amid hot US CPI inflation, US-Iran tensions
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair trades on a flat note near 1.3695 during the early Asian trading hours on Wednesday. Traders continue to assess hot US inflation data and ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The US April Producer Price Index (PPI) report will take center stage later on Wednesday.

articles.title: PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8431 (vs. estimate at 6.7946)
articles.expand: The PBOC allows the yuan to fluctuate within a +/- 2% range, around this reference rate. Injects 500mn yuan via 7-day reverse repos in open market operates today. Unchanged rate of 1.4%. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

The pair set its peak during the Asian and early European hours, then fell to a session low around the 0.5935 area mid-session, before recovering part of the move into the New York close.

articles.title: Dunkin’ Donuts coming back to Canada with hundreds of locations
articles.expand: Dunkin’ Donuts officially left the Canadian market in 2018, closing its last locations in Quebec. The first location is expected to open in late 2026 or early 2027.
